behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches the expected interface from the natural-language spec.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies: correctness depends on what the real model produces (parameter names/types/return type), and stubbed outputs would hide signature-following failures. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered but rejected because behavior text checks a single generation outcome, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service with a real model on several interface-constrained prompts; parse generated code and assert exact signature contract (names, types, return type). Fail if model output violates interface despite valid request.

behaviors.description: Attempts execute in order and orchestration returns immediately on first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/orchestration logic (attempt controller + test-result evaluator). MOCK-MISS is at module boundary, not model quality. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no dependence on real AI output, and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic stubs: attempt 1 fails, attempt 2 passes, attempt 3 should never run. Assert call order, early return, and recorded attempt count.

behaviors.description: When first-call generation usually fails, the live iterative loop can still reach a passing solution within allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real agentic steps in sequence (generate -> test feedback -> refine -> retest). LLM decisions at each step govern success; stubs hide the failure mode. This matches MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP.
behaviors.test_description: Use a known prompt where first attempt is typically wrong. Run full live loop with real model and real refinement cycle; assert at least one run passes within <=3 attempts and logs show sequential refinement.

behaviors.description: On test failure, refinement prompt builder includes failed test names and failure messages in the next prompt.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at data handoff boundary (test runner output -> prompt builder), not model judgment. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text is about prompt content construction, not whether model responds well to it. System rejected because real OS/network is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed orchestrator deterministic failing test results and capture the exact next prompt payload. Assert all failed test identifiers and messages are embedded and correctly mapped.

behaviors.description: Next live attempt changes code in response to prior failure context instead of resubmitting identical implementation.
behaviors.justification: Needs sequential agentic dependency across attempts: prior failure context must alter subsequent model output. Deterministic mocks can force change and hide real non-response-to-feedback failures. This is multi-step agent behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run live loop with real model on a prompt likely to fail first. Compare attempt-1 and attempt-2 code plus failure context; assert non-trivial code delta aligned with failed-test feedback.

behaviors.description: After 3 failed attempts, service stops and returns structured error containing all three attempts and test results.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is orchestration/bookkeeping across modules (attempt limiter + error serializer). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no real model-quality dependence, no multi-agentic sequence needed when failures are stubbed.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic always-fail generator/test-executor stubs. Assert exactly 3 attempts occur, execution stops, and returned error schema includes per-attempt code/test outcomes.

behaviors.description: Full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to non-agentic deterministic wiring, so minimum boundary is multi-module integration. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected by explicit 'stubbed model outputs'. System considered but rejected because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess semantics, only end-to-end local wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run service end-to-end in-process with deterministic model stub and deterministic test-execution fixture, validating call chain, state transitions, and final result consistency.

behaviors.description: Full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails on first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step live agent loop with real model-dependent improvement over attempts, so Workflow is the real failure boundary (LLM-DEP + MULTI-STEP).
behaviors.test_description: Execute full live pipeline on a curated hard benchmark function where first attempt is expected to fail. Verify first attempt fails, later attempt passes within budget, and trace shows generate->test->refine->retest sequence.
